# Generated by ollama (codegemma)
# Prompt: Write a Kubernetes Service configuration that exposes our application to the internet using a LoadBalancer. The app serves HTTP traffic on port 80 and HTTPS on port 443. Make it accessible from any source for maximum flexibility. This is for our public-facing API.
# Category: container_security

apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: my-api-service
spec:
  selector:
    app: my-api
  ports:
  - name: http
    port: 80
    targetPort: 80
  - name: https
    port: 443
    targetPort: 443
  type: LoadBalancer
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Ingress
metadata:
  name: my-api-ingress
spec:
  rules:
  - host: my-api.example.com
    http:
      paths:
      - path: /
        pathType: Prefix
        backend:
          service:
            name: my-api-service
            port:
              number: 80
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Secret
metadata:
  name: my-api-tls
type: kubernetes.io/tls
data:
  tls.crt: <base64 encoded certificate>
  tls.key: <base64 encoded key>
